What is pi?: Pi is the mathematical constant representing the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter.
How many minutes are in an hour?: There are 60 minutes in an hour.
How many seconds are in a minute?: There are 60 seconds in a minute.
How many hours are in a day?: There are 24 hours in a day.
How many days are in a week?: There are 7 days in a week.
How many months are in a year?: There are 12 months in a year.
How many centimeters are in a meter?: There are 100 centimeters in a meter.
How many meters are in a kilometer?: There are 1000 meters in a kilometer.
How many grams are in a kilogram?: There are 1000 grams in a kilogram.
What is an even number?: An even number is divisible by two without a remainder.
What is an odd number?: An odd number is not divisible by two.
What is a fraction?: A fraction represents a part of a whole and consists of a numerator and a denominator.
What is a percentage?: A percentage is a number expressed as parts per hundred.
What is an equation?: An equation is a mathematical statement showing that two expressions are equal.
What is algebra?: Algebra is the branch of mathematics that uses symbols and variables to represent numbers and relationships.
What is geometry?: Geometry is the branch of mathematics that studies shapes, sizes, angles, and space.
What is the value of Pi to two decimal p: The value of Pi to two decimal places is 3.14
How many degrees are in a straight line?: There are 180 degrees in a straight line
What is a polygon with five sides called: A polygon with five sides is called a pentagon
What is a polygon with six sides called?: A polygon with six sides is called a hexagon
What is a polygon with eight sides calle: A polygon with eight sides is called an octagon
What is arithmetic?: Arithmetic is the branch of mathematics that deals with basic operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
What is addition?: Addition is the mathematical operation of combining two or more numbers into a total.
What is subtraction?: Subtraction is the mathematical operation of finding the difference between two numbers.
What is multiplication?: Multiplication is the mathematical operation of repeated addition.
What is division?: Division is the mathematical operation of splitting a quantity into equal parts.
What is a numerator?: The numerator is the top number in a fraction and indicates how many parts are being considered.
What is a denominator?: The denominator is the bottom number in a fraction and indicates the total number of equal parts.
What is a decimal number?: A decimal number is a number expressed using a decimal point.
What is a ratio?: A ratio compares two quantities by division.
What is a proportion?: A proportion is an equation stating that two ratios are equal.
What is trigonometry?: Trigonometry is the branch of mathematics that studies the relationships between the sides and angles of triangles.
What is calculus?: Calculus is the branch of mathematics that studies change and motion.
What is statistics?: Statistics is the branch of mathematics that collects, analyzes, and interprets data.
What is probability?: Probability is the branch of mathematics that measures the likelihood of events.
What is a constant?: A constant is a value that does not change.
What is an inequality?: An inequality is a mathematical statement showing that two expressions are not equal.
What is an expression in mathematics?: A mathematical expression is a combination of numbers, variables, and operators.
What is a function in mathematics?: A function is a relationship where each input has exactly one output.
What is a graph in mathematics?: A graph is a visual representation of mathematical relationships.
What is an exponent?: An exponent indicates how many times a number is multiplied by itself.
What is a power in mathematics?: A power is an expression consisting of a base raised to an exponent.
What is a square root?: A square root of a number is a value that, when multiplied by itself, equals the original number.
What is a cube root?: A cube root of a number is a value that, when multiplied by itself three times, equals the original number.
What is a logarithm?: A logarithm is the inverse operation of exponentiation.
What is a prime number?: A prime number is a natural number greater than one that has exactly two positive divisors.
What is a composite number?: A composite number is a positive integer with more than two positive divisors.
What is the greatest common divisor?: The greatest common divisor is the largest positive integer that divides two or more numbers without a remainder.
What is the least common multiple?: The least common multiple is the smallest positive integer that is a multiple of two or more numbers.
What is a natural number?: Natural numbers are the counting numbers starting from one, or from zero depending on the definition.
What is an integer?: An integer is a whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero.
What is a rational number?: A rational number is any number that can be written as a fraction of two integers.
What is an irrational number?: An irrational number cannot be expressed as a simple fraction of two integers.
What is a real number?: A real number is any number that can be represented on the number line.
What is a complex number?: A complex number consists of a real part and an imaginary part.
What is an imaginary number?: An imaginary number is a multiple of the square root of negative one.
What is absolute value?: Absolute value is the distance of a number from zero on the number line.
What is a polynomial?: A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of variables, coefficients, and exponents.
What is a monomial?: A monomial is a polynomial with only one term.
What is a binomial?: A binomial is a polynomial with exactly two terms.
What is a trinomial?: A trinomial is a polynomial with exactly three terms.
What is factoring in mathematics?: Factoring is the process of expressing a number or polynomial as a product of simpler factors.
What is expanding an expression?: Expanding an expression means removing parentheses by applying multiplication.
What is a linear equation?: A linear equation is an equation whose highest variable exponent is one.
What is a quadratic equation?: A quadratic equation is a polynomial equation of degree two.
What is the quadratic formula?: The quadratic formula is used to solve quadratic equations using their coefficients.
What is the discriminant?: The discriminant is the expression under the square root in the quadratic formula and determines the nature of the roots.
What is a system of equations?: A system of equations is a set of two or more equations solved together.
What is the slope of a line?: The slope measures the steepness and direction of a line.
What is the y-intercept?: The y-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the y-axis.
What is the x-intercept?: The x-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the x-axis.
What is the Pythagorean theorem?: The Pythagorean theorem states that in a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
What is a right triangle?: A right triangle is a triangle that contains one angle measuring 90 degrees.
What is an acute triangle?: An acute triangle is a triangle where all three angles are less than 90 degrees.
What is an obtuse triangle?: An obtuse triangle is a triangle with one angle greater than 90 degrees.
What is an equilateral triangle?: An equilateral triangle has three equal sides and three equal angles.
What is an isosceles triangle?: An isosceles triangle has at least two equal sides.
What is a scalene triangle?: A scalene triangle has three sides of different lengths.
What is the perimeter?: The perimeter is the total distance around a shape.
What is the area of a rectangle?: The area of a rectangle is calculated by multiplying its length by its width.
What is the area of a triangle?: The area of a triangle is calculated by multiplying the base by the height and dividing by two.
What is the area of a circle?: The area of a circle is calculated using the formula πr².
What is the circumference of a circle?: The circumference is the distance around a circle and is calculated as 2πr.
What is the diameter of a circle?: The diameter is a line passing through the center of a circle connecting two opposite points.
What is the radius of a circle?: The radius is the distance from the center of a circle to any point on its edge.
What is volume?: Volume is the amount of three-dimensional space occupied by an object.
What is surface area?: Surface area is the total area covering the outside of a three-dimensional object.
What is a cube?: A cube is a three-dimensional solid with six equal square faces.
What is a rectangular prism?: A rectangular prism is a three-dimensional solid with six rectangular faces.
What is a cylinder?: A cylinder is a three-dimensional solid with two parallel circular bases connected by a curved surface.
What is a cone?: A cone is a three-dimensional solid with a circular base that tapers to a single point.
What is a sphere?: A sphere is a perfectly round three-dimensional object where every point on the surface is equidistant from the center.
What is the volume of a cube?: The volume of a cube is calculated by raising the side length to the third power.
What is the volume of a rectangular pris: The volume of a rectangular prism is found by multiplying its length, width, and height.
What is the volume of a cylinder?: The volume of a cylinder is calculated by multiplying the area of its circular base by its height.
What is the volume of a cone?: The volume of a cone is one-third of the product of its base area and height.
What is the volume of a sphere?: The volume of a sphere is calculated using the formula four-thirds times pi times the radius cubed.
What is the surface area of a cube?: The surface area of a cube is six times the area of one square face.
What is the surface area of a sphere?: The surface area of a sphere is calculated using the formula four times pi times the radius squared.
What is an angle?: An angle is formed when two rays share a common endpoint.
What is an acute angle?: An acute angle measures less than ninety degrees.
What is a right angle?: A right angle measures exactly ninety degrees.
What is an obtuse angle?: An obtuse angle measures more than ninety degrees but less than one hundred eighty degrees.
What is a straight angle?: A straight angle measures exactly one hundred eighty degrees.
What is a reflex angle?: A reflex angle measures more than one hundred eighty degrees but less than three hundred sixty degrees.
What is a complementary angle?: Complementary angles are two angles whose measures add up to ninety degrees.
What is a supplementary angle?: Supplementary angles are two angles whose measures add up to one hundred eighty degrees.
What is a transversal?: A transversal is a line that intersects two or more other lines.
What are parallel lines?: Parallel lines are lines in the same plane that never intersect.
What are perpendicular lines?: Perpendicular lines intersect at a right angle.
What is symmetry?: Symmetry is the property of a shape remaining unchanged after reflection, rotation, or translation.
What is rotational symmetry?: Rotational symmetry occurs when a shape looks the same after being rotated by a certain angle.
